results.possibleIdentity: Edward Samuels was a technical specialist and engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, specializing in lighting technology. He was employed by the Tube Light Company (now known as Tube Light Company, Inc.), a sign manufacturing firm based on East Park Street in Moonachie, New Jersey. Samuels is recognized for his collaboration with artists through the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which aimed to facilitate partnerships between the technical and artistic communities. Most notably, he provided engineering expertise to the neon artist Victor Millonzi. Their collaborative project was featured in the E.A.T.-organized exhibition titled "Some More Beginnings: An Exhibition of Submitted Works Involving Technical Materials and Processes," which was held at the Brooklyn Museum in late 1968. This exhibition was a major showcase of works where engineers from industrial companies, such as the Tube Light Company, applied their professional knowledge to creative art installations.
